assertion_results.text: Step 1 extracts imprecise affected range from the Jira description — the description lacks a specific version threshold (Step 1 Data Extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md explicitly marks affected version range as 'versions prior to the fix (imprecise)' and fixed version as 'see advisory (imprecise)' in the extracted fields table (lines 12-13). The Data Quality Notes section (lines 44-46) reiterates: 'Affected version range is imprecise: "versions prior to the fix" -- no specific version threshold provided in the Jira description' and 'Fixed version is imprecise: "see advisory" -- no specific version number in the Jira description'. Step 1 correctly identifies and flags the lack of a specific version threshold.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries MITRE CVE API and extracts lessThan 0.4.8 as the fix threshold from the affected[].versions[] field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md section 1 shows query to 'https://cveawg.mitre.org/api/cve/CVE-2026-48901' and parsed data includes 'Affected versions: lessThan 0.4.8 (semver)' with version type 'semver' and status 'affected' (lines 6-10). Line 12 states 'The MITRE CVE record provides a structured lessThan constraint: all versions below 0.4.8 are affected. This means version 0.4.8 is the fix threshold.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 queries OSV.dev API and extracts fixed version 0.4.8 from the affected[].ranges[].events field (§1.62)
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md section 2 shows query to 'https://api.osv.dev/v1/vulns/CVE-2026-48901' and parsed data includes 'Range type: SEMVER', 'Introduced: 0 (all versions from the beginning)', 'Fixed: 0.4.8' (lines 21-23). Line 25 confirms 'The OSV.dev record confirms that version 0.4.8 is the fix point. All versions from 0 up to (but not including) 0.4.8 are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.5 cross-validation shows agreement between MITRE and OSV.dev — the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) is used as the authoritative value for Step 2.3
assertion_results.evidence: enrichment.md section 3 (Cross-Validation) provides a comparison table of Jira, MITRE, and OSV.dev sources (lines 29-34). Line 36 states 'The MITRE CVE API and OSV.dev agree on the fix threshold: 0.4.8.' Lines 40-42 declare 'Enriched fix threshold: 0.4.8' and 'This enriched threshold will be used in Step 2.3 for version impact comparisons. Versions shipping h2 < 0.4.8 are affected; versions shipping h2 >= 0.4.8 are not affected.'

assertion_results.text: Step 2.3 version impact comparison uses the enriched fix threshold (0.4.8) from Step 1.5, not the imprecise Jira description data
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 5 explicitly states 'Using enriched fix threshold from Step 1.5: h2 < 0.4.8 (MITRE and OSV.dev agree).' The version impact table (lines 15-20) uses 0.4.8 as the comparison threshold throughout (e.g., '0.4.5 < 0.4.8' -> YES affected, '0.4.8 >= 0.4.8' -> NO). No reference to imprecise Jira description data ('versions prior to the fix' or 'see advisory') is used for version comparison. The threshold is attributed to Step 1.5 external enrichment, not the Jira description.
